name: Eldee Young
description: American jazz musician; double-bass and cello player (1936-2007)
abstract: Eldee Young (January 7, 1936 – February 12, 2007) was a jazz double-bass and cello player who performed in the cool jazz, post bop and rhythm and blues mediums. Born in 1936 in Chicago, Illinois, Young started playing upright bass at the age of 13.

concerts.label: Ramsey Lewis Trio, Flip Wilson, and the Herbie Mann Octet
Teddy Powell's Holiday Soul of Jazz
Midnight Performance: Teddy Powell's Holiday Soul of Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: Eldee Young played bass. ... Eldee and I were left. ... We were playing at this club called the S.R.O. Room ... with Eldee and I
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
